sourceMovie.title: Rare Exports: A Christmas Tale
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Rare Exports: A Christmas Tale works as a fantasy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The fantasy and horror blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Krampus
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Rare Exports: A Christmas Tale clicked for you, Krampus (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Rare Exports: A Christmas Tale?
faq.answer: Try Krampus, Nightbreed, Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets—each shares DNA with Rare Exports: A Christmas Tale while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Rare Exports: A Christmas Tale?
faq.answer: Rare Exports: A Christmas Tale (2010) is best described as Fantasy, Horror. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Rare Exports: A Christmas Tale worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this fantasy lane.
